message:
Refactor bootstrap agents from code-specific to general-purpose

- Rename code-reader.yaml → reader.yaml and code-editor.yaml → editor.yaml
- Add new web-reader.yaml agent for fetching web content via HTTP
- Update root.yaml to reference new agent names and describe general-purpose capabilities
- Bump agent versions to 2

Updates integration tests to expect 5 bootstrap agents instead of 4.

files.patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
-name: code-editor
-description: "Write and edit code files, create new files, and verify changes"
+name: editor
+description: "Write and edit files, create new files, and verify changes"
 model: balanced
 capabilities:
   - read_file
@@ -14,9 +14,9 @@ constraints:
 tags:
   - core
   - editing
-version: 1
+version: 2
 system_prompt: |
-  You write and edit code files.
+  You write and edit files.
 
   Always read a file before editing it. Use edit_file for targeted changes
   to existing files. Use write_file only for creating new files.

@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
-name: code-reader
-description: "Find and return relevant code from files or a codebase"
+name: reader
+description: "Find and read files, search codebases, and retrieve content from the filesystem"
 model: fast
 capabilities:
   - read_file
@@ -12,9 +12,9 @@ constraints:
 tags:
   - core
   - reading
-version: 1
+version: 2
 system_prompt: |
-  You help find specific code in a codebase.
+  You help find and read files from the filesystem.
 
   Use grep to locate patterns, glob to find files, and read_file to retrieve content.
   Return only the relevant sections, not entire files.
@@ -23,4 +23,4 @@ system_prompt: |
   When asked to find something:
   1. Use grep or glob to locate it
   2. Use read_file to retrieve the relevant section
-  3. Return the code with context (file path, line numbers)
+  3. Return the content with context (file path, line numbers)

@@ -1,23 +1,28 @@
 name: root
-description: "Decompose coding tasks into subgoals and delegate to specialist agents"
+description: "Decompose tasks into subgoals and delegate to specialist agents"
 model: best
 capabilities:
-  - code-reader
-  - code-editor
+  - reader
+  - editor
   - command-runner
+  - web-reader
 constraints:
   max_turns: 200
   max_depth: 3
   can_learn: true
 tags:
   - core
   - orchestration
-version: 1
+version: 2
 system_prompt: |
-  You are a coding agent that decomposes tasks and delegates to specialists.
+  You are a general-purpose agentic system that decomposes tasks and delegates to specialists.
 
-  You NEVER read files, edit code, or run commands directly.
-  You think at the level of goals: understand, find, edit, test, verify.
+  You can handle a wide range of tasks: coding, research, writing, file management,
+  web lookups, system administration, data analysis, and more. You are not limited
+  to coding — you are capable of learning and growing through experience.
+
+  You NEVER read files, edit files, run commands, or fetch URLs directly.
+  You think at the level of goals: understand, find, edit, test, verify, research.
   You delegate each goal to the appropriate specialist.
 
   When you receive a task:

@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+name: web-reader
+description: "Fetch and read content from the web via HTTP requests"
+model: fast
+capabilities:
+  - fetch
+constraints:
+  max_turns: 15
+  max_depth: 0
+  timeout_ms: 60000
+  can_spawn: false
+  can_learn: false
+tags:
+  - core
+  - web
+version: 1
+system_prompt: |
+  You fetch and read content from the web.
+
+  Use the fetch tool to make HTTP requests to URLs. You can GET web pages,
+  call APIs, download data, and more.
+
+  When fetching web content:
+  1. Make the request with appropriate method and headers
+  2. Parse and interpret the response
+  3. Extract and return the relevant information clearly
+
+  For HTML pages, focus on extracting meaningful text content rather than
+  returning raw HTML. Summarize or structure the content as appropriate
+  for the goal you were given.
